column3: PXE is used for network boot and remote installation workflows.
column4: The acronym is useful, but the decision target is recognizing when the device should boot from the network.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Network Boot Source::Messer-v140

column3: GPT is the newer partition style used with modern UEFI systems.
column4: Keep the acronym separate from the selection rule: GPT is the modern choice for large or many partitions.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Partitioning Source::Messer-v140

column3: MBR is the older partition style with older partition-size and partition-count limits.
column4: The acronym is direct recall. The practical exam decision is usually GPT versus MBR limitations.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Partitioning Source::Messer-v140

column3: UEFI support is part of the GPT selection decision for modern installations.
column4: This is a compact prerequisite recall card. The related Basic card asks when to choose GPT.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 3
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Partitioning UEFI Source::Messer-v140

column2: A quick format creates a new {{c1::file table}} without overwriting every sector.
column3: A full format writes zeros across the disk and checks for bad sectors.
column4: This helps distinguish "looks empty" from actually overwriting the disk.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Format Source::Messer-v140

column2: Windows quality updates focus on {{c1::security updates and bug fixes}}.
column3: Quality updates are different from feature updates, which add new features.
column4: This distinction matters when planning upgrade timing and lifecycle support.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
column9: A+::220-1202::1.2 A+::220-1202::Domain1-OperatingSystems A+::220-1202::OSInstallationsandUpgrades Cloze Updates Source::Messer-v140

column3: Feature updates are separate from monthly quality updates.
column4: Use the name as the clue: feature updates introduce features, while quality updates focus on fixes.
column8: CompTIA 220-1202 Exam Objectives v4.0, Objective 1.2; Professor Messer 220-1202 v1.40, p. 4
